utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 keys. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: must be physical and mental health insurance with costs. Provided: physical 'LifeFirst Standard Plan (€100/month)' and mental 'InsurePlus Standard Plan (€60/month)'. Fully specified with costs. 2) property_coverage: must be home/contents insurance with costs. Provided: 'HomeFirst Standard Coverage (€55/month)'. It mentions home but not explicitly contents; however the format says 'home/contents insurance with costs' which can be interpreted as home insurance; costs are included. Acceptable. 3) travel_coverage: must be travel insurance with costs. Provided: 'InsurePlus Standard Plan (€50/month) + Adventure Travel Coverage (€80/month)'. Costs included. 4) additional_services: any enhancement services with costs. Provided: 'Adventure Travel Coverage (€80/month)'. Costs included; although duplicated with travel, it still meets requirement. 5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided: monthly €345 and annual €4140. Therefore all 5 items are present and include costs; they are sufficiently specified per the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Extract items from the final plan and match them to identically named options in the ground-truth. Mappings found: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) rating 9 under primary_health_coverage.physical_health; InsurePlus Standard Plan (mental health) rating 8 under primary_health_coverage.mental_health; HomeFirst Standard Coverage rating 8 under property_protection.home_content; InsurePlus Standard Plan (travel insurance) rating 10 under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance; Adventure Travel Coverage rating 10 under travel_enhancement_services. The assistant duplicated Adventure Travel Coverage in both travel_coverage (as an add-on) and additional_services; both map to the same item and rating.
